\title{\Large \bf \titleText}
\author{{\large Test Author}\thanks{Test affiliation.}}
\date{{\large January 1, 2026}}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
\noindent
This is a sample paper used to test the LaTeX-to-annotated-markdown converter.
It contains sections, math, figures, tables, and citations.
\end{abstract}

\noindent \textbf{Keywords:} testing, converter, sample

\section{Introduction}
\label{sect:intro}

This paper tests whether the converter handles basic LaTeX constructs.
We cite \citealp{AuthorA2025} and also \citet{AuthorB2026}.

\section{Data and Methods}
\label{sect:data}

\paragraph{Data Sources.}
We use sample data from 2020 to 2025. The key variable is $X_t$.

\paragraph{Implied Measures.}
Implied variance is:
\begin{align}
    IV_t = \sum_i \frac{\Delta K_i}{K_i^2} Q(K_i).
\end{align}\label{eq:iv}
Realized variance is:
\begin{align}\label{eq:rv}
    RV_t = \sum_{t}^{T-1} r_{t,t+1}^2.
\end{align}

\section{Unconditional Results}
\label{sect:uncond}

Strategy PNL is highly variable. We report results in Table~\ref{tab:main}.

\subsection{Robustness}
\label{sect:robust}

Results survive multiple-testing correction.\footnote{We use Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control.}

\section{Conditional Signals}
\label{sect:cond}

The conditional model predicts:
\begin{align}
    p_{s,t} = \Pr(y^{net}_{s,t} > 0 \mid X_{s,t}) = \Lambda(\alpha_s + \beta_s^\top X_{s,t}).
\end{align}
Hard mapping: $w_t = \mathrm{sign}(p_t - 0.5)$.

\begin{table*}[!htbp]
\centering
\input{\tables wide_table.tex}
\caption{\textbf{Model Comparison.} Comparing different models.}
\label{tab:wide}
\end{table*}

The converter handles sections, math, figures, tables, and citations.
